query: France Ebola case 2026: current status of the doctor who tested positive after aid mission, any secondary cases, ECDC reporting. How many confirmed Ebola cases in France as of July 2026?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here is the key finding:

**As of July 2026, France has exactly ONE (1) confirmed Ebola case** — an imported case in a humanitarian doctor (with the NGO ALIMA) who returned from the DRC, confirmed on 24 June 2026. The doctor **recovered and was discharged on 4 July 2026** after two negative PCR tests, per Health Minister Stéphanie Rist. The patient was nearly asymptomatic throughout.

**There have been ZERO secondary cases.** Five airplane contacts were placed in precautionary isolation but none became infected. ECDC (page last updated 6 July 2026) rates EU/EEA infection likelihood as "very low." The case stemmed from the ongoing Bundibugyo-strain outbreak in DRC/Uganda (a WHO PHEIC), which totaled ~1,561 cases/506 deaths in DRC and 20 cases/2 deaths in Uganda as of 5 July 2026.

sources.title: Ebola disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda
sources.snippet: As of 5 July 2026: DRC 1,561 confirmed cases, 506 deaths; Uganda 20 cases, 2 deaths. One imported case in France; EU/EEA likelihood of infection considered very low.

sources.title: France's first Ebola case recovered and discharged from hospital
sources.snippet: Health Minister Stéphanie Rist announced the doctor recovered after two negative PCR tests and was discharged on 4 July 2026; five airplane contacts were isolated as a precaution; no secondary cases.

sources.title: First Ebola case confirmed in France, health ministry says
sources.snippet: France confirmed its first Ebola case, a doctor returned from a humanitarian mission in the DRC — the first Ebola case confirmed in Europe.

sources.title: Ebola response worker who traveled from DRC to France is first in outbreak to test positive outside of Africa
sources.snippet: First reported case in France and first to test positive outside Africa amid the second-largest Ebola outbreak on record in DRC, driven by the rare Bundibugyo strain.

sources.title: Ebola Arrives in France: Infected Aid Doctor Flew Commercial as DRC Cases Mount
sources.snippet: France confirmed its first Ebola case on 24 June 2026 — a physician with the humanitarian organization ALIMA who had been treating patients in the DRC.

sources.title: Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, DRC & Uganda (Disease Outbreak News)
sources.snippet: 1,481 confirmed cases; 1,460 from DRC, 20 from Uganda and one from France (linked to DRC); 454 deaths reported.

sources.title: 2026 Ebola epidemic
sources.snippet: 17th DRC Ebola outbreak declared 14 May 2026 in Ituri Province; caused by Bundibugyo ebolavirus; PHEIC declared by WHO 16 May 2026; imported case reported in France.

sources.title: France confirms first case of Ebola in doctor who had worked in Congo
sources.snippet: Doctor admitted to a special health facility, in stable condition; health workers tracing possible contacts.

sources.title: Ebola virus outbreak 2026 - Public Health - European Commission
sources.snippet: WHO determined on 17 May 2026 that the Bundibugyo virus outbreak in DRC and Uganda constitutes a PHEIC; ECDC involved in coordinated response.

key_facts: France confirmed its first-ever Ebola case on national territory on 24 June 2026 — an imported case, not locally acquired.
The case was a humanitarian doctor working with the NGO ALIMA who returned from the DRC, flying commercially (Air France) on 23 June 2026.
It was the first Ebola case ever confirmed in France and the first case in the 2026 outbreak to test positive outside Africa.
The patient was almost asymptomatic (only headaches) and in stable condition; treated in isolation under strict biosafety protocols.
The doctor recovered and was discharged on 4 July 2026 after two negative PCR tests, announced by Health Minister Stéphanie Rist.
ZERO secondary cases have occurred in France or the EU/EEA.
Five fellow airplane passengers were identified as possible contacts and placed in precautionary isolation; none became infected.
As of July 2026, France has exactly ONE (1) confirmed Ebola case in total.
ECDC assesses the likelihood of infection for people in the EU/EEA as 'very low'; its outbreak page was last updated 6 July 2026.
The outbreak is caused by Bundibugyo ebolavirus, declared a WHO PHEIC on 16–17 May 2026; the French case was counted in WHO tallies as linked to DRC.
Broader outbreak (as of 5 July 2026): ~1,561 confirmed cases / 506 deaths in DRC; 20 cases / 2 deaths in Uganda.

data_points.metric: Airplane contacts of French case placed in precautionary isolation
